issuesFound.fix: Line 1824: .social-links a { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-2); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-300); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); min-height: 44px; padding-block: var(--space-2); }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on account navigation
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove text-decoration and rely solely on color change (to var(--colour-brand-primary)) on hover, failing WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color as there is no non-color visual differentiator like underline, border, or font-weight change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 723: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:hover { color: var(--colour-brand-primary); text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on footer navigation
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have text-decoration: none and only change color on hover (to var(--colour-neutral-0)), providing no non-color visual indicator that the text is a link or that it is being hovered.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1810: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:hover { color: var(--colour-neutral-0); text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border. This removes the keyboard focus ring for all interactive elements that do not override this rule, making keyboard navigation impossible to track.
issuesFound.fix: Line 335: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on @media (forced-colors: active) :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The forced-colors media query sets outline: none on :focus-visible without a visible replacement. This breaks focus visibility for users relying on high-contrast modes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 347: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid CanvasText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset but provides no box-shadow or border. Keyboard users focusing on buttons will see no focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 466: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector removes the outline without a visible alternative. The mobile navigation toggle will have no focus ring for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 516: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links have outline: none in their :focus-visible state with no replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify focus within dropdown menus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 558: [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes its outline on focus without a box-shadow or border replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when this button is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 613: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the outline on focus without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot track focus in the account menu.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn min-height 32px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8). This makes buttons difficult to activate on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is significantly below the 24px minimum and 44px recommended size. Small buttons are hard to tap accurately.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon min-width/min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width and min-height to 20px. Icon buttons must be at least 24px (AA) or 44px (AAA) to be accessible.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .nav-toggle min-height/min-width 28px

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"] width/height 28px

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a min-height 24px

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] width/height 20px

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility fails to hide content
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static and clip: auto, which renders content visibly instead of hiding it. This breaks the intended screen-reader-only pattern.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class uses transform transitions, but there is no prefers-reduced-motion override to disable this motion for users who request it.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img

issuesFound.title: Button transform transition missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (line 294) which causes spatial movement on active states, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this animation for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Hero image transform animation missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector includes a transform transition and transform property that creates spatial movement on hover, and the corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block on line 420 is empty, failing to suppress the animation for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` function adds the open class and announces the state change but fails to set `aria-expanded='true'` on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the navigation is now expanded, as only the `close()` function sets the attribute to false.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu._openInstance()` function removes the hidden attribute and adds the open class but fails to set `aria-expanded='true'` on the trigger button. This leaves the ARIA state out of sync with the visual state, confusing assistive technology users.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open()` function expands the answer panel and announces the change but fails to set `aria-expanded='true'` on the question trigger. Only the `close()` function updates this attribute, causing state desynchronization for screen readers.

issuesFound.title: productFilter _syncTabAriaStates function does not update aria-selected
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter._syncTabAriaStates()` function toggles the visual active class on filter tabs but does not update the `aria-selected` attribute. Keyboard and screen reader users rely on this attribute to identify the currently active filter tab.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() omits aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() utility function adds the error element to aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Without this attribute, screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users. The markValid() function correctly removes aria-invalid, but since it is never set, the invalid state persists incorrectly.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2672: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` as the first line inside the markInvalid() function, before the addDescribedBy() call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function adds CSS classes to display the mobile navigation drawer and announces the state via liveRegion, but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not know the menu is expanded. The close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false".
issuesFound.fix: Line 1764: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the `_open = true;` assignment inside the open() function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not trap focus inside the drawer when open
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable for focus trapping but never initializes or activates a focus trap in the open() function. When the drawer is open, keyboard users can tab out of it and access the background content behind the overlay, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator creates error elements with conflicting ARIA roles
issuesFound.explanation: The _getOrCreateErrorEl() function creates error message spans with both role="alert" and aria-live="polite". The alert role implies an assertive live region, while aria-live="polite" requests a polite interruption. This contradiction causes unpredictable screen reader behavior.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3164: Remove the `'aria-live': 'polite'` property from the createElement() call for the error element, keeping only `role: 'alert'` (which implicitly provides aria-live="assertive").

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() fails to set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened, the _openInstance() function removes the hidden attribute from the panel and emits an event, but does not update the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". The _closeInstance() function correctly sets it to "false", leaving the state out of sync when open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1924: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the beginning of the _openInstance() function, immediately after the `inst.open = true;` assignment.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function animates the FAQ panel and updates internal state, but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", so screen readers will report the item as collapsed even when it is visually expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3424: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;` inside the open() function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu panels lack focus trapping
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened, focus is not trapped within the panel. While keyboard navigation (arrow keys) is supported, a user can Tab out of the dropdown, causing it to remain open while focus moves to the next focusable element on the page. The panel should either trap focus or close when focus leaves.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play button missing aria-pressed initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause button in the carousel uses aria-pressed to indicate state, but the initial state is not explicitly set when the carousel initializes. The play() and pause() functions set it, but on init, the button may not reflect the correct initial pressed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3630: In the init() function of testimonialsCarousel, after creating the play button reference, add `setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', String(_playing));` to synchronize the initial state.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function adds an aria-describedby reference to the error element but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers rely on aria-invalid to announce that a field is in an error state. Without this attribute, users may not be notified that their input is invalid even if the error message is linked.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function removes the hidden attribute from the panel and adds the open class, but it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The corresponding _closeInstance function correctly sets aria-expanded="false". This mismatch leaves assistive technology users unaware that the dropdown is now expanded.

issuesFound.title: User action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function closes the navigation drawer and sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger, but it does not announce the state change to screen readers via a live region. The open function announces "Navigation menu opened", but closing is silent, leaving users unsure if the action succeeded.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The cookieConsent._showBanner function displays the consent dialog and traps focus, but it does not announce the banner's appearance to screen readers. Users may be confused by the sudden focus change without context.

Line 652 (inside markInvalid function, after addDescribedBy):

issuesFound.explanation: When implementing a tab list pattern (used by pricing period buttons and filter tabs), the function updates tabindex but fails to set aria-selected="true" on the active tab and aria-selected="false" on inactive tabs. Screen readers cannot identify which tab is active.

issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile nav closes, the drawer may remain in the accessibility tree if CSS uses transforms to position it off-screen. The function does not set aria-hidden="true" to remove it from the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons use aria-selected without role="tab"
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (Monthly/Annual) use aria-selected to indicate state, but they are native buttons or lack role="tab". Buttons should use aria-pressed for toggle state, or they must have role="tab" with role="tablist" on the container.

issuesFound.title: characterCounter lacks aria-describedby linkage

issuesFound.title: statCounterAnimator._animate function updates content without live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The _animate function dynamically updates the text content of stat elements to count up numbers but does not announce the final value or the change to an aria-live region. Screen reader users may miss the updated statistic entirely.

issuesFound.title: Form validation missing aria-invalid updates
issuesFound.explanation: When form fields are marked invalid or valid, the aria-invalid attribute is not set to true or false. Screen readers cannot convey the validation state to users, violating WCAG 4.1.2.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu triggers do not update aria-expanded when panels open or close. Assistive technologies cannot announce the expanded state of submenu panels to users.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close missing live region announcement

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle missing aria-expanded updates

issuesFound.title: productFilter missing live region announcement on filter change
issuesFound.explanation: When product filters are applied, the result count updates visually but is not announced to screen readers. Users do not receive confirmation that filtering occurred or how many results are showing.

issuesFound.title: pricing period change missing live region announcement

issuesFound.fix: Line pricing.setPeriod(): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged ? STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged(period) : \`Prices switched to \${period} billing\`);` immediately after `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period });`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion missing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing FAQ items does not announce the state change to screen readers. Users are not informed when answers are revealed or hidden.
issuesFound.fix:

issuesFound.title: siteSearch missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The search input combobox does not initialise aria-expanded to false on page load. Assistive technologies may incorrectly report the suggestion list as expanded when it is closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line siteSearch.init(): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-describedby', srUtils.ensureId(_statusEl));`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, the focus trap is never created or activated, allowing keyboard focus to escape to background elements behind the drawer. This violates WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function inside the `mobileNav` module toggles the visibility of the navigation drawer (`_drawer`) via CSS classes but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button (`_trigger`). Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is now expanded.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `close` function inside the `mobileNav` module hides the navigation drawer but fails to set `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button (`_trigger`). Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is now collapsed.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init function does not initialise aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function inside the `mobileNav` module sets up event listeners but does not set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute on the trigger (`_trigger`) if the menu is closed on load. This leaves the state undefined for assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` function inside the `dropdownMenu` module removes the `hidden` attribute from the panel but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger element (`inst.trigger`). This desynchronizes the ARIA state from the visual state.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `_closeInstance` function inside the `dropdownMenu` module hides the panel but fails to set `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger element (`inst.trigger`). This desynchronizes the ARIA state from the visual state.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function inside the `faqAccordion` module expands the answer panel but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the question trigger (`item.trigger`). Note that the `close` function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state management.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `markInvalid` function in `srUtils` links the error message via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field itself. Screen readers rely on `aria-invalid` to announce that a field is in an error state.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markValid function does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `markValid` function in `srUtils` removes the error description but fails to remove the `aria-invalid` attribute (or set it to "false"). The field may remain stuck in an invalid state for assistive technologies even after correction.

issuesFound.title: productFilter count badge update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_updateCountBadge` function in `productFilter` updates the text content of the product count badge when filters change, but the element is not configured as a live region (no `aria-live`), and no announcement is made. Users won't know how many results match their filter.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onClick` handler in `scrollToTop` scrolls the page to the top and moves focus, but does not announce the action via the live region. Users may be disoriented by the sudden viewport change without confirmation.

issuesFound.title: productFilter roving tabs missing Enter/Space activation
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in `productFilter` initialises roving tabindex for filter tabs with `activateOnFocus: false` but does not add a keydown listener for Enter or Space to trigger the selection. Keyboard users can focus tabs but cannot activate them.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters function updates the visible product grid (hiding/showing cards) based on user selection but does not announce the result (e.g., "Showing X products") via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2625: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory));

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce price change
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function updates all price displays on the page when the billing period changes but does not announce this significant content change to screen readers via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2910: liveRegion.announce(period === 'monthly' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly : STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual);

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop click action not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollToTop._onClick function scrolls the page to the top but does not announce this action to screen readers. Users may lose context of where they are on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2063: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.scrolledToTop);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance functions change visible content (show/hide menus) but do not announce the change to screen readers via a live region.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1788: liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');
Line 1805: liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function retrieves the trigger element but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on it. Screen readers may not identify it as a collapsible control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1675: if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a form field as invalid, the function adds the error element to aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true". Screen readers cannot identify the field as having an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1222: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, the function adds CSS classes to show the drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1853: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters does not announce filter results via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the visible product count updates and the grid changes, but no announcement is made to screen readers about the results (e.g., "Showing 5 products"). Users are unaware the content has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3318: Add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);` after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When closing the mobile navigation, the function removes CSS classes but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, leaving screen readers with stale state information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1872: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` at the start of the function

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod does not announce billing period change via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When users toggle between monthly and annual billing, the prices update visually but screen readers receive no announcement of the change (e.g., "Prices switched to annual billing").
issuesFound.fix: Line 3865: Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);` after updating the toggle UI

issuesFound.title: siteSearch suggestionListRenderer.hide removes aria-expanded instead of setting to false
issuesFound.explanation: When hiding the suggestion list, the function removes the aria-expanded attribute entirely instead of setting it to "false". This leaves the combobox in an indeterminate state for screen readers rather than explicitly indicating the list is collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2785: Change `_inputEl.removeAttribute('aria-expanded');` to `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When clearing validation errors, the function removes the error from aria-describedby but does not remove the aria-invalid attribute or set it to "false", leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1240: Add `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` at the end of the function

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open does not activate focus trap or move focus into drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The open function declares a focus trap variable `_trap` but never activates it or moves keyboard focus into the drawer. Keyboard users remain on the trigger button while the drawer opens visually.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1858: Add `if (_trap) _trap.activate();` and `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0]; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The function opens the dropdown panel by removing the hidden attribute and adding CSS classes, but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button to indicate the expanded state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2053: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The function closes the dropdown panel but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, leaving assistive technologies unaware that the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2072: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` at the start of the function

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates does not set aria-pressed on filter tab buttons
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons act as toggle buttons (showing active/inactive states) but the function only toggles CSS classes without setting aria-pressed="true" or "false". Screen readers cannot convey which filter is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3355: Add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open does not announce expanded state via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded, the content becomes visible but screen readers are not notified. The STRINGS.faqOpened template exists but is never invoked.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4258: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.title));` after setting the open state

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close does not announce collapsed state via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is collapsed, screen readers receive no confirmation that the content has been hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4282: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed(item.title));` after setting the closed state

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not announce menu open via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu opens, keyboard and screen reader users receive no confirmation that the menu is now available for navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2055: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` after opening the panel

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance does not announce menu close via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu closes (especially on Escape key), users are not notified that the menu is no longer available.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2080: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` before closing the panel

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger elements not updating aria-expanded dynamically
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu trigger buttons (inst.trigger) have aria-expanded set to "false" on initialization but are not updated to "true" when the dropdown opens or "false" when it closes dynamically.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1835: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
Line 1853: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function fails to set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion open function removes the hidden attribute from the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to false, causing a state mismatch where the trigger always appears collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2975: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Assistive technologies rely on aria-invalid to announce that a field is in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1295: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid utility function removes the error description but does not remove or set aria-invalid="false" on the field. Fields remain marked as invalid to assistive technologies even after successful validation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1307: field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid'); field.removeAttribute('aria-describedby');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav open function announces "Navigation menu opened" via liveRegion, but the close function does not announce that the menu has closed. Screen reader users are not informed when the navigation overlay disappears.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1695: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing FAQ items changes visible content but does not trigger a live region announcement. Screen reader users are not notified when an answer expands or collapses.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2975: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened || 'Answer expanded');
Line 3005: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed || 'Answer collapsed');

issuesFound.title: productFilter count badge updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product count badge text is updated, but this change is not announced to screen readers via an aria-live region. Users are unaware of how many results match their filter.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2295: _countBadge.textContent = String(count); liveRegion.announce(`${count} products found`);

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly and annual billing updates the visible prices but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users may not realize the prices have updated to reflect the new billing period.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2515: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, the function adds the error message to aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers do not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix:
Function `markInvalid()`: Add after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`
  `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens or closes, no announcement is made to screen reader users via a live region. Users are not informed that the navigation context has changed.
issuesFound.fix:
Function `open()`: Add after `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);`
  Function `close()`: Add after `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`
  `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing dropdown menus changes the visible content but does not announce the state change to assistive technologies, leaving screen reader users unaware that a panel has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Function `_openInstance()`: Add after `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown opened');`
  Function `_closeInstance()`: Add after `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`
  `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown closed');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on triggers
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu triggers use the aria-expanded pattern to indicate panel visibility, but the functions that open and close the panels do not programmatically update this attribute on the trigger element.
issuesFound.fix:
Function `_openInstance()`: Add after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`
  `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Function `_closeInstance()`: Add after `if (!inst.open) inst.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`
  `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing FAQ items visually reveals content, but screen reader users receive no confirmation that the action succeeded or that new content is available.
issuesFound.fix:
Function `open()`: Add after `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.title));`
  Function `close()`: Add after `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`
  `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.title));`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on triggers
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion triggers manage expandable panels but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute when the panel state changes dynamically. While initialization sets the initial state, subsequent toggles do not reflect the current state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Function `open()`: Add after `addClass(item.el, CSS.open);`
  `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Function `close()`: Add after `removeClass(item.el, CSS.open);`
  `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing, the price values update visually but the change is not announced to screen reader users, leaving them unaware that the pricing context has changed.
issuesFound.fix:
Function `setPeriod()`: Add after `_updateSavingsMessage(period);`
  `liveRegion.announce(period === 'month' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly : STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When validation errors are corrected, the function removes the error description but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute, leaving the field marked as invalid to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Function `markValid()`: Add at the end of the function (after the if/else block for aria-describedby)
  `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The category filter tab buttons indicate the active filter via CSS classes but do not set aria-pressed to communicate the selected state to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: productFilter changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and the product grid updates, the change in result count and category is not announced to screen reader users, who may not realize the content has changed.
issuesFound.fix:
Function `applyFilters()`: Add after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`
  `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(_productFilterState.activeCategory));`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded initialized to "false" on page load when the menu is closed, violating the requirement that toggle buttons must reflect their initial state.
issuesFound.fix:
Function `init()`: Add after `_initialised = true;`
  `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`
  `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown triggers are registered without initializing aria-expanded to "false", leaving assistive technologies unaware that the element controls a collapsible panel.

Function `register()`: Add after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: Form validation errors are visually indicated and linked via aria-describedby, but the aria-invalid attribute is never set on invalid fields or cleared on valid fields. Screen readers cannot identify invalid fields programmatically, violating WCAG 4.1.2 and 3.3.1.
issuesFound.fix: In inlineValidator._renderResult, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, String(!result.valid));` or update srUtils.markInvalid to include `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true);` and srUtils.markValid to include `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu triggers do not update aria-expanded when their panels are opened or closed. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the state change to users, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In dropdownMenu._openInstance: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` and in dropdownMenu._closeInstance: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: productFilter missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and product cards are shown/hidden, no announcement is made to screen readers. Users are not informed of the updated result count or current filter, violating WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages.
issuesFound.fix: At the end of applyFilters (inside the filterAnimation.run callback), add: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied ? STRINGS.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : \`Showing ${totalMatches} products\`);`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When opening an FAQ item, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not set to "true" (though it is correctly set to "false" when closing). This leaves screen readers unaware that the panel is expanded, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In faqAccordion.open, after `item.open = true;`, add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);`

issuesFound.title: pricing missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When switching billing periods, the price changes are not announced to screen readers. Users are unaware that the displayed prices have updated, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: At the end of pricing.setPeriod, add: `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button does not have aria-expanded="false" set on initial page load. Screen readers assume the menu is expanded by default, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: After `_trigger.addEventListener('keydown', _onTriggerKeydown);`, add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing FAQ items changes visible content but does not announce the state change to screen readers. The announceToSR parameter exists but is never used to trigger announcements, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: In faqAccordion.open: add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.title));` and in faqAccordion.close: add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed(item.title));`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button lacks aria-controls pointing to the drawer element. Screen readers cannot identify which element is controlled by the button, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: After ensuring _drawer has an id (e.g., `srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav')`), add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, _drawer.id);`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox input does not have aria-expanded="false" set on initialization. Screen readers assume the suggestion list is visible by default, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: After `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`, add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: When opening the mobile navigation, the focus trap is created but never activated, and focus is not moved to the drawer. Keyboard users remain on the toggle button and cannot access the menu contents, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order.
issuesFound.fix: At the end of mobileNav.open, add: `if (_trap) _trap.activate(); const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing dropdown menus changes visible content but does not announce the state change to screen readers, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: In dropdownMenu._openInstance: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Menu opened');` and in dropdownMenu._closeInstance: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing the mobile navigation drawer changes the page layout significantly but does not announce the state change to screen readers, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.open: add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');` and in mobileNav.close: add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` and `mobileNav.close()` functions toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer and the `nav-open` class on the body, but they do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the `_trigger` button. Screen reader users will not be informed when the menu state changes.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init does not initialise aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init()` function locates the `_trigger` element but does not set an initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute. If the menu is closed on load, the trigger should explicitly report its collapsed state.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu._openInstance()` and `dropdownMenu._closeInstance()` functions show and hide the panel but fail to update the `aria-expanded` attribute on `inst.trigger`. This breaks the disclosure pattern for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register does not initialise aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu.register()` function sets `aria-haspopup` and `aria-controls` on the trigger but does not set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` state. The trigger's state is ambiguous on page load.

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `pricing._syncToggleState()` function updates the visual active state of billing period buttons but does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute. The selected period is not conveyed to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch does not update aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The `pricing` module assigns `role="switch"` to `_toggleEl` in `init()`, but `_syncToggleState()` and `_onToggleClick()` only toggle the `CSS.checked` class. The `aria-checked` state is never updated, so the switch state is not announced.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open()` function expands the answer panel but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the `item.trigger`. The `close()` function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markInvalid()` function links the error message via `aria-describedby` but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field. Screen readers may not announce the field as invalid.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markValid()` function removes the error description but does not remove or set `aria-invalid="false"`. The field may remain stuck in an invalid state for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop click handler lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The `scrollToTop._onClick()` function scrolls the page to the top but does not announce this action to screen readers via a live region. Users may be disoriented by the sudden viewport change.

issuesFound.title: productFilter empty state change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter._updateEmptyState()` function shows or hides the empty state message based on the product count but does not announce this change to screen readers. Users may not know why the grid is empty.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The utility function links error messages via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Assistive technologies cannot programmatically determine the field's invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1450: Insert `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after the line `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);` inside the markInvalid function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open function adds CSS classes to show the drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state to users, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2140: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `_open = true;` inside the open function.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open function animates the panel and removes the hidden attribute but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Although the close function sets it to false, the mismatch means the expanded state is never announced.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3350: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `item.open = true;` inside the open function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation close function removes CSS classes to hide the drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. The missing state change prevents assistive technologies from announcing the collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2155: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after the line `_open = false;` inside the close function.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._open() does not update aria-expanded on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestion panel open function updates internal state and shows suggestions but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the input element (role="combobox"). Screen readers cannot announce that the suggestion list is visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2920: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `_searchState.open = true;` inside the _open function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates() does not update aria-pressed on filter tabs
issuesFound.explanation: The category filter tab buttons toggle active CSS classes but do not set aria-pressed, preventing screen readers from announcing which filter is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3090: Insert `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` immediately after the line `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` inside the _syncTabAriaStates function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu opens, the function removes the hidden attribute and adds CSS classes but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves screen reader users unaware that the menu is visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2250: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `inst.open = true;` inside the _openInstance function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu closes, the function removes CSS classes and sets the hidden attribute but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, so the collapsed state is not conveyed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2262: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after the line `inst.open = false;` inside the _closeInstance function.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the function removes error descriptions but leaves aria-invalid="true" set, causing screen readers to continue announcing the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1460: Insert `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` at the end of the markValid function, before the closing brace.

issuesFound.title: pricing._syncToggleState() does not update aria-pressed on period buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (month/quarter/year) act as a single-select group but only toggle CSS classes. They lack aria-pressed state, so screen readers cannot convey which period is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3180: Insert `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` immediately after the line `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` inside the _syncToggleState function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init() does not initialise aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation initialiser sets up event listeners but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button when the page loads, violating the requirement that toggle buttons indicate their initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2180: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after the line `_initialised = true;` inside the init function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register() does not initialise aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown pair, the function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but omits aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, leaving the initial collapsed state unannounced.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2320: Insert `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after the line `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');` inside the register function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Opening the mobile navigation drawer is a significant context change that is not announced via a live region, leaving screen reader users unaware that the menu has appeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2140: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');` immediately before the line `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', { drawer: _drawer });` inside the open function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Closing the mobile navigation drawer is not announced, so users navigating with screen readers may not realise the menu has closed and focus has returned to the trigger.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2155: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` immediately before the line `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer });` inside the close function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown menu changes visible content but lacks a live region announcement, preventing screen reader users from knowing the menu is now available.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2250: Insert `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` immediately before the line `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel });` inside the _openInstance function.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not announce expanded state to screen readers

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod() does not announce billing period change to screen readers

issuesFound.fix: Line 2293: <FormGroup label="Subject" htmlFor="contact-subject" required errorId="contact-subject-error" errorMessage={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav section trigger buttons missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The buttons toggle the visibility of navigation subsections but lack aria-expanded attributes, preventing screen reader users from knowing whether a section is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <button aria-expanded={isExpanded} aria-controls={sectionId} ...>

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The filter buttons act as toggle buttons where one is always pressed/active, but they lack aria-pressed attributes to communicate the active state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <button aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} ...>

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion trigger has aria-controls linking to the answer panel but no aria-expanded attribute, so screen readers cannot announce the panel's current expanded or collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <button aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-controls={panelId} ...>

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation landmark containing skip links lacks an aria-label to identify its purpose in the landmark structure.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <nav aria-label="Skip links">

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel navigation buttons missing accessible names
issuesFound.explanation: The previous and next navigation buttons use IconButton without providing the required aria-label prop, resulting in buttons that have no accessible name for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1: <IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} ...>
  Fix: Line 2: <IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} ...>

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link is visually styled with a distinct class but lacks aria-current="page" to programmatically indicate the current page to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay clickable div missing button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The overlay has an onClick handler to close the modal but lacks role="button", tabIndex, and keyboard event handlers, making it inaccessible to keyboard users and unrecognized as interactive by assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Change to: <button type="button" className="modal-overlay" aria-label="Close modal" onClick={onClose}> or add role="button", tabIndex={0}, and onKeyDown handler for Enter and Space keys.

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute incorrectly placed on overlay
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal attribute is placed on the overlay element instead of the dialog element with role="dialog", which may cause assistive technology to incorrectly handle the modal's modality and focus scope.
issuesFound.fix: Remove aria-modal from the overlay div and add aria-modal="true" to the div with role="dialog".

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop to style the input when there is an error, but it does not pass the aria-invalid attribute to the underlying <input> element. Screen reader users will not be informed that the field contains an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 392: <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: Textarea component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an invalid prop to style the textarea when there is an error, but it does not pass the aria-invalid attribute to the underlying <textarea> element. Screen reader users will not be informed that the field contains an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 412: <textarea id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-textarea', invalid ? 'form-textarea--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email field error not associated via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput has aria-describedby pointing to 'newsletter-email-error', but FormGroup is not passed the errorId prop, so the error message element lacks this ID. Additionally, FormGroup expects errorMessage prop but receives error. Screen readers cannot announce the error message.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1: Change FormGroup props to `errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} errorId="newsletter-email-error"`. Line 2: Ensure ErrorMessage inside FormGroup uses the errorId prop as its id.

issuesFound.title: SiteHeader mobile menu toggle missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile hamburger menu button controls the visibility of the navigation panel but lacks aria-expanded to indicate whether the menu is currently open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button: `<button id={toggleId} ref={menuToggleRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-label={isOpen ? 'Close navigation menu' : 'Open navigation menu'} className="mobile-nav-toggle" onClick={isOpen ? close : open}>`

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter buttons act as toggle buttons showing the active filter state but do not use aria-pressed to communicate this state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value}: `<button type="button" className={...} aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>`

issuesFound.fix: Line 1442: <button type="button" id="billing-annual" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>

issuesFound.title: StatsBar section missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The StatsBar section landmark lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby. While it contains a visually hidden heading, the section element itself does not reference it, making navigation difficult for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1135: <section aria-labelledby="stats-heading" className="stats-bar">

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks nav missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation element containing skip links lacks an aria-label. When multiple nav landmarks exist, each must have a unique accessible name to distinguish them.
issuesFound.fix: Line 335: <nav aria-label="Skip links">

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav nav missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The primary desktop navigation landmark lacks an aria-label. It is not distinguished from other nav elements (e.g. mobile nav, footer nav) for assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 655: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main navigation" className="desktop-nav">

issuesFound.title: MobileNav body nav missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation element inside the mobile panel lacks an aria-label. It is not distinguished from other nav elements for assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 775: <nav aria-label="Mobile navigation" className="mobile-nav__body">

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The article element representing a pricing plan lacks aria-labelledby. It should reference the heading inside it (id={headingId}) to provide a clear accessible name for the card.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1485: <article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker announces asterisk
issuesFound.explanation: The visual required field marker (*) is not hidden from assistive technology. It may be read aloud as "asterisk" which is confusing. It should have aria-hidden="true".
issuesFound.fix: Line 525: *

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy when loading
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component accepts an isLoading prop but does not apply aria-busy="true" to the button element. Users are not informed that the button is in a busy/loading state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 413: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading ? 'true' : undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: Spinner inner ring missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The decorative inner ring of the Spinner component is not hidden from assistive technology. It should have aria-hidden="true" as the parent already has role="status".
issuesFound.fix: Line 470: <span className="spinner__ring" aria-hidden="true" />

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav active links missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links indicating the current page (item.current) lack aria-current="page". Assistive technology users cannot easily identify which page they are currently on.
issuesFound.fix: Line 665: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav active links missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links in the mobile menu indicating the current page (item.current) lack aria-current="page". Assistive technology users cannot easily identify which page they are currently on.
issuesFound.fix: Line 795: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={onClose}>

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar clone list missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The cloned list of integration logos used for visual looping is not hidden from assistive technology. It creates duplicate content for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1225: <ul role="list" aria-hidden="true" className="integrations-bar__list">

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown chevron missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The decorative chevron icon (▲/▼) inside the NavDropdown trigger is not hidden from assistive technology. It conveys no additional information beyond the button state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 638: {isOpen ? '▲' : '▼'}

issuesFound.title: Skip navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <nav> element wrapping the skip links lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby attribute. Screen reader users navigating by landmarks cannot distinguish this navigation region from others on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 406: <nav aria-label="Skip navigation">

issuesFound.title: Base Button component missing aria-busy state during loading
issuesFound.explanation: The <button> element inside the Button component does not toggle aria-busy when the isLoading prop is true. Assistive technology users are not informed that the button is busy processing a request.
issuesFound.fix: Line 399: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading || undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: Required field marker announces asterisk to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The <span> containing the asterisk (*) required marker lacks aria-hidden="true". Screen readers will announce "asterisk" or "star" unnecessarily, as the required state is already conveyed by the input's required attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 537: *

issuesFound.title: Desktop navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <nav id="main-nav"> element lacks an aria-label attribute. When multiple nav landmarks exist, each must have a unique label to allow differentiation by assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 626: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main navigation" className="desktop-nav">

issuesFound.title: Active desktop navigation link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation link uses a className to indicate the current page but lacks aria-current="page". Screen reader users are not explicitly informed which link represents the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 630: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: Theme toggle icon button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton used for the theme toggle does not receive an aria-label prop. As an icon-only button, it has no text content, making it inaccessible to screen reader users without an explicit label.
issuesFound.fix: Line 808: <IconButton onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} aria-label={LABELS[theme]} className="theme-toggle">

issuesFound.title: Hero section landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <section> element for the hero area lacks an aria-labelledby or aria-label attribute. Users navigating by landmarks cannot identify this section's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 883: <section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">

issuesFound.title: Stats bar section landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <section className="stats-bar"> element lacks an aria-labelledby attribute referencing the internal h2#stats-heading. The section landmark is not properly named for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 963: <section aria-labelledby="stats-heading" className="stats-bar">

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation body landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <nav className="mobile-nav__body"> element lacks an aria-label attribute. It should be distinguished from other navigation landmarks on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 748: <nav aria-label="Mobile navigation menu" className="mobile-nav__body">

issuesFound.title: Active mobile navigation link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation link indicates the current page via className but lacks aria-current="page". Assistive technology users are not informed of the current page location.
issuesFound.fix: Line 773: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={onClose}>

issuesFound.title: Pricing plan card article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <article> element inside the pricing card lacks aria-labelledby referencing the internal heading id. Screen reader users navigating by articles cannot identify the plan name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1267: <article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">

issuesFound.title: Pricing plan CTA link missing descriptive accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The CTA link text (e.g., "Start Pro free trial") is generic when multiple plans exist. It lacks an aria-label that includes the specific plan name to ensure uniqueness.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1295: <a href={plan.ctaHref} aria-label={`${plan.ctaLabel} — ${plan.name} plan`} className={['btn', plan.featured ? 'btn--primary' : 'btn--outline', 'btn--md'].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.fix: Line 1438: <IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel next button missing accessible name
